Condition: PHQ-9 or GAD-7 score change < 3 points from baseline
Recommendation: Consider treatment modification: reassess diagnosis, adjust modality, discuss medication evaluation, or increase session frequency. Review therapeutic alliance.
Evidence_Source: APA Practice Guidelines for MDD (2019); NICE CG90 — Treatment-resistant depression pathway

Recommendation: Immediate clinical attention. Conduct suicide risk assessment. Consider medication evaluation if not already prescribed. Discuss higher level of care (IOP/PHP) if appropriate.
Evidence_Source: Kroenke et al. (2001) PHQ-9 validation; APA Guidelines recommend combined therapy + pharmacotherapy for severe depression

Condition: PHQ-9 Item 9 score >= 2 (more than half the days or nearly every day)
Recommendation: Immediate safety assessment required. Complete or update safety plan. Evaluate need for higher level of care. Document risk assessment and clinical decision-making.
Evidence_Source: Columbia Suicide Severity Rating Scale protocol; Joint Commission National Patient Safety Goal 15.01.01

Condition: PHQ-9 or GAD-7 score increase >= 5 points from previous session
Recommendation: Assess for acute stressors or precipitating events. Review medication adherence. Consider safety assessment if depression worsening. Schedule follow-up within 1 week.
Evidence_Source: Reliable Change Index (Jacobson & Truax 1991); clinically significant worsening threshold

Condition: 2 or more missed appointments (no-show or late cancel) in lookback window
Recommendation: Engagement outreach indicated. Contact patient to assess barriers. Explore ambivalence about treatment. Consider scheduling flexibility or telehealth if transportation is a barrier.
Evidence_Source: Swift & Greenberg (2012) meta-analysis on premature termination; Barrett et al. (2008) predictors of dropout

Condition: Session notes indicate patient reports stopping or skipping medication without prescriber guidance
Recommendation: Discuss medication concerns with patient. Coordinate with prescribing provider. Explore barriers (side effects cost stigma). Psychoeducation about discontinuation risks.
Evidence_Source: NIMH STEP-BD findings on medication adherence; Velligan et al. (2017) adherence interventions

Condition: PHQ-9 or GAD-7 score stable (change < 2 points) across lookback window after initial improvement
Recommendation: Treatment may have reached ceiling with current approach. Consider adding skills-based component, adjusting session frequency, medication augmentation, or transitioning to maintenance phase if scores are in mild range.
Evidence_Source: Cuijpers et al. (2019) dose-response in psychotherapy; Howard et al. (1986) dose-effect model

Recommendation: Severe anxiety range. Assess functional impairment and panic symptoms. Consider medication evaluation if not already prescribed. Ensure evidence-based anxiety protocol is being used (CBT with exposure components).
Evidence_Source: Spitzer et al. (2006) GAD-7 validation; APA Clinical Practice Guideline for Anxiety Disorders

Condition: Session notes indicate new or increased substance use
Recommendation: Screen with AUDIT-C or DAST-10. Assess interaction with current medications. Consider integrated treatment or referral to substance use specialist. Evaluate impact on treatment progress.
Evidence_Source: SAMHSA TIP 42 — Substance Use Disorder Treatment for People with Co-Occurring Disorders

Condition: Patient has completed 20+ sessions without reaching mild symptom range
Recommendation: Comprehensive treatment review indicated. Consider case consultation, diagnostic reassessment, modality change, or referral to specialist. Document clinical rationale for continued treatment.
Evidence_Source: Lambert et al. (2001) expected treatment response curves; Hansen et al. (2002) dose-response findings

Condition: PHQ-9 or GAD-7 score decrease >= 50% from baseline within first 4 sessions
Recommendation: Positive prognostic indicator. Monitor for sustained improvement. Begin relapse prevention planning early. Consider whether reduced frequency is appropriate.
Evidence_Source: Lutz et al. (2009) early response as predictor of outcome; Haas et al. (2002) early improvement research

Condition: Patient has 2+ concurrent risk factors: severe score AND missed appointment AND medication non-adherence
Recommendation: Elevated clinical concern. Prioritize outreach and safety planning. Consider care coordination call between therapist and psychiatrist. Evaluate need for higher level of care.
Evidence_Source: Fowler (2012) risk factor accumulation model; Bryan & Rudd (2006) clinical management of suicidal patients
